      "It is a Grade I listed building."

      "It has offices for courtroom staff.",

    "IsA": [

      "それはヨーロッパで最大級の裁判所の一つである。",
      "それはグレードI指定建築物である。"
    ],

      "それは1階に窓枠のある5連窓を持つ破風を持つ。",

      "それはヴィクトリア女王の胸像がある。"

      "それはヴィクトリア朝ゴシック・リバイバル様式によって特徴づけられる。",

      "それは大ホールに通じるアーチ型の入り口によって特徴づけられる。",
      "それは1階の彫刻された枠にある5連窓によって特徴づけられる。",
      "それはバラ窓のある破風によって特徴づけられる。",

      "それはピラミッド型の屋根を持つ高い時計塔によって特徴づけられる。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それはイングランドおよびウェールズ高等法院を収容するために使用される。",
      "それはイングランドおよびウェールズ控訴院を収容するために使用される。",
      "それはCOVID-19パンデミック中の「ナイチンゲールコート」として刑事裁判に使用された。",
      "それは大ホールから配置された法廷のために使用される。"
    ],

      "それはウェストミンスター市内に位置する。",
      "それはシティ・オブ・ロンドンの境界近くに位置する。",
      "それはウェストミンスターに位置する。"
    ],

      "それは1990年1月に完成したトーマス・モア・コートとともに建てられた。"

IsA: It is one of the largest courts in Europe.
It is a Grade I listed building.
HasA: It has a gable containing a rose window.
It has towers containing lancet windows.
It has a courtyard to the east.
It has offices for courtroom staff.
It has a bust of Queen Victoria.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its grey stone edifice.
It is characterized by the Victorian Gothic Revival style.
It is characterized by a symmetrical main frontage.
It is characterized by a central section that is stepped back.
It is characterized by an arched doorway leading to the Great Hall.
It is characterized by a five-part window in a carved surround on the first floor.
It is characterized by a gable containing a rose window.
It is characterized by a sculpture of Jesus above the gable.
It is characterized by statues of Moses, Solomon and Alfred the Great.
It is characterized by a tall clock tower topped by a pyramidal roof.
UsedFor: It is used for housing the High Court of England and Wales.
It is used for housing the Court of Appeal of England and Wales.
It is used for criminal trials as a 'Nightingale Court' during the COVID-19 pandemic.
It is used for courtrooms arranged off the Great Hall.
LocatedIn: It is located on The Strand.
It is located within the City of Westminster.
It is located near the boundary with the City of London.
BuiltIn: It was built in eight years.
It was built with the West Green building completed in 1912.
It was built with the Queen's Building completed in 1968.
It was built with the Thomas More Courts completed in January 1990.
BuiltBy: It was built by Messrs Bull & Sons of Southampton.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the 19th century when justices decided courts needed a purpose-built structure.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a central section, side wings, and towers.
It consists of a Great Hall.
It consists of offices for courtroom staff.
It consists of a tall clock tower.
It consists of five bells weighing a total of 8¼ tons.
KnownFor: It is known for housing the High Court and Court of Appeal of England and Wales.
It is known for its Victorian Gothic Revival style.
It is known for its large grey stone edifice.
It is known for being one of the largest courts in Europe.
It is known for being a Grade I listed building.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with George Edmund Street.
It is associated with Queen Victoria.
It is associated with Edwin Wilkins Field.
It is associated with Messrs Bull & Sons of Southampton.
It is associated with Sir Henry Tanner.
It is associated with Nikolaus Pevsner.
It is associated with David Brownlee.
It is associated with Turnor.
It is associated with Henry Tanworth Wells.
It is associated with Banksy.
HeldIn: It was held in the 19th century.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the reformist political movement.
It was influenced by the High Victorian architectural movement.
